compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, the planning cascade after brainstorm terminates safely with a configurable cycle cap
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Defines the post-brainstorm cascade protocol: brainstorm revises the epic, preplanning re-runs to revise stories, implementation-plan re-runs to realign tasks. The cascade terminates on confidence or after a configurable max cycle count.
**Why**: Without a termination condition, the self-healing loop could oscillate indefinitely between planning tiers. Without context file invalidation, cascaded re-runs load stale pre-revision data.
**Scope**:
- IN: sprint.max_replan_cycles config key in dso-config.conf (default 2), cascade termination logic, preplanning context file invalidation before cascade re-run, cascade protocol documentation
- OUT: Sprint orchestrator cascade invocation logic (separate epic 9d3e-957d), brainstorm mid-implementation mode, preplanning re-invocation logic (already exists)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, a sprint.max_replan_cycles key exists in dso-config.conf with default value 2, and the cascade protocol documentation references it as the termination condition
  ← Satisfies: "after sprint.max_replan_cycles (configurable in dso-config.conf, default 2) cascade iterations"
- When this story is complete, the cascade protocol specifies that the preplanning context file (/tmp/preplanning-context-<epic-id>.json) must be invalidated before preplanning re-run to prevent stale pre-revision context from being loaded
  ← Satisfies: "preplanning re-runs... then implementation-plan re-runs to realign tasks"
- When this story is complete, the cascade protocol is documented with clear entry/exit conditions so that the sprint detection epic can implement the invocation logic
  ← Satisfies: "The cascade terminates when implementation-plan produces a plan it can execute with confidence"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Reliability] Context file invalidation timing is critical — stale context file must be cleared before preplanning re-run
- [Maintainability] Cascade protocol must be documented clearly enough for the sprint detection epic (9d3e-957d) to implement without ambiguity

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
